narration: Partition Labels first records the last position of every letter.
callout: Those last positions tell how long a partition must stay open.

narration: While scanning, the current partition end stretches to the farthest last position of any letter seen so far.
decision: When the scan reaches that end, the partition can safely close.

predictionPrompt.prompt: When is it safe to cut the current partition?

choices.label: When the current index reaches the farthest needed end
choices.feedback: Correct. That means every letter in the partition is finished.

choices.label: As soon as a letter repeats
choices.feedback: Not this one. A repeat can force the partition to stay open longer.

narration: Greedy cuts at the earliest safe spot so the string gets as many parts as possible.
callout: That earliest safe cut is the greedy move.
